<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="UTF-8">
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
<title>Laweh Wah</title>
<style>
  body {
    font-family: Georgia, "Times New Roman", serif;
    max-width: 780px;
    margin: 0 auto;
    padding: 48px 28px 80px;
    background: #ffffff;
    color: #1a1a1a;
    font-size: 16px;
    line-height: 1.7;
  }

  /* --- Header --- */
  .header {
    border-bottom: 3px solid #1a1a1a;
    padding-bottom: 20px;
    margin-bottom: 12px;
  }
  .header h1 {
    font-size: 38px;
    font-weight: bold;
    letter-spacing: -0.5px;
    margin: 0 0 6px;
  }
  .header .role {
    font-size: 16px;
    color: #444;
    margin: 0 0 12px;
    font-style: italic;
  }
  .header .contact-bar {
    font-size: 14px;
    color: #555;
    font-family: Arial, sans-serif;
  }
  .header .contact-bar a {
    color: #1a4fa0;
    text-decoration: none;
  }
  .header .contact-bar a:hover { text-decoration: underline; }
  .header .contact-bar span { margin: 0 8px; color: #bbb; }

  /* --- Section headings --- */
  h2 {
    font-size: 13px;
    text-transform: uppercase;
    letter-spacing: 0.12em;
    color: #888;
    font-family: Arial, sans-serif;
    font-weight: 700;
    margin: 72px 0 20px;
    border-bottom: 1px solid #e0e0e0;
    padding-bottom: 8px;
  }

  /* --- Body text --- */
  p {
    margin: 0 0 14px;
    color: #2a2a2a;
  }

  /* --- Projects --- */
  .project {
    margin-bottom: 48px;
    padding-bottom: 48px;
    border-bottom: 1px solid #e8e8e8;
  }
  .project:last-child {
    border-bottom: none;
    margin-bottom: 0;
    padding-bottom: 0;
  }
  .project h3 {
    font-size: 19px;
    margin: 0 0 3px;
    letter-spacing: -0.2px;
  }
  .project .meta {
    font-family: Arial, sans-serif;
    font-size: 13px;
    color: #777;
    margin: 0 0 12px;
  }
  .project img {
    width: 100%;
    display: block;
    margin: 16px 0;
    border: 1px solid #ddd;
    border-radius: 3px;
  }
  .project a.readmore {
    font-family: Arial, sans-serif;
    font-size: 13px;
    color: #1a4fa0;
    text-decoration: none;
    font-weight: 600;
  }
  .project a.readmore:hover { text-decoration: underline; }

  /* --- Skills --- */
  .skills-table {
    width: 100%;
    border-collapse: collapse;
  }
  .skills-table tr {
    border-bottom: 1px solid #efefef;
  }
  .skills-table td {
    padding: 10px 0;
    font-size: 15px;
    vertical-align: top;
  }
  .skills-table td:first-child {
    font-family: Arial, sans-serif;
    font-weight: 700;
    font-size: 13px;
    color: #444;
    width: 160px;
    padding-right: 20px;
  }

  /* --- Education --- */
  .edu-item {
    display: flex;
    gap: 24px;
    margin-bottom: 28px;
    align-items: baseline;
  }
  .edu-item .years {
    font-family: Arial, sans-serif;
    font-size: 13px;
    color: #888;
    min-width: 110px;
    flex-shrink: 0;
  }
  .edu-item .details h3 {
    font-size: 17px;
    margin: 0 0 2px;
  }
  .edu-item .details p {
    margin: 0;
    font-size: 14px;
    color: #555;
    font-family: Arial, sans-serif;
  }
  .edu-item .details .gpa {
    color: #1a4fa0;
    font-weight: 700;
  }

  /* --- Contact footer --- */
  .contact-footer {
    margin-top: 64px;
    padding: 24px 28px;
    background: #f8f8f8;
    border-left: 3px solid #1a4fa0;
    border-radius: 2px;
  }
  .contact-footer p {
    margin: 0 0 10px;
    font-size: 15px;
  }
  .contact-footer p:last-child { margin: 0; }
  .contact-footer a {
    color: #1a4fa0;
    text-decoration: none;
  }
  .contact-footer a:hover { text-decoration: underline; }
</style>
</head>
<body>

<!-- Header -->
<div class="header">
  <h1>Laweh Wah</h1>
  <p class="role">Electrical &amp; Computer Engineering Student &mdash; University of Washington</p>
  <div class="contact-bar">
    <a href="mailto:lwah@uw.edu">lwah@uw.edu</a>
    <span>|</span>
    <a href="tel:+15095086838">+1 509-508-6838</a>
    <span>|</span>
    Seattle, WA
    <span>|</span>
    Open to internships
  </div>
</div>

<!-- About -->
<h2>About</h2>
<p>
  I'm an ECE student at the University of Washington who wants to do controls and automation. Before college I
  led a team building an underwater trash-collection robot for SkillsUSA Washington State,
  and I went through a 150-hour machinist program in Spokane. I tend to learn best when
  there's something to build.
</p>
<p>
  I'm actively looking for an internship in controls,
  automation, embedded systems, or robotics.
</p>

<!-- Projects -->
<h2>Projects</h2>

<div class="project">
  <h3>Non-Linear Liquid Level Control System Design</h3>
  <p class="meta">MATLAB &middot; Simulink &middot; PID &middot; Bode Analysis &middot; Root Locus &middot; Spring 2025</p>
  <p>
    Tank drains by gravity, so the math is non-linear — I worked out the plant model
    from scratch, then simplified it enough to tune a PID controller around.
    Hit <strong>1.78% overshoot</strong>, 10.1s rise time, 15.1s settling time, and an
    <strong>88.2° phase margin</strong>.
  </p>
  <p>
    Checked stability with a Bode plot and root locus, then ran the tuned controller
    against the full non-linear model to make sure it actually held up.
  </p>
  <img src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Simulink block diagram — Non-Linear Tank Plant">
  <a href="https://docs.google.com/document/d/17NMoWcBZfLYXlcJJNvXn06MD5D7TT0_lj_R2ShCQ5gg/edit?usp=sharing" target="_blank" class="readmore">Read full write-up &rarr;</a>
</div>

<div class="project">
  <h3>Automated Sorting Line: Ladder and Control Logic</h3>
  <p class="meta">CODESYS &middot; IEC 61131-3 Ladder Logic &middot; Factory I/O &middot; OPC UA &middot; Ignition Perspective &middot; Spring 2025</p>
  <p>
    Built a height-based box sorting line from the ground up: CODESYS SoftPLC running
    IEC 61131-3 ladder logic, connected to a Factory I/O 3D simulation over OPC UA,
    with an Ignition Perspective HMI for live monitoring through a web browser.
  </p>
  <p>
    The parts I found most interesting to work out: pipelining the belt traffic so boxes
    keep moving without backing up, using Set/Reset latches to hold each box's type in
    memory as it travels down the line, and adding motor interlocks so the rollers and
    pop-up chains can't conflict with each other.
  </p>
  <img src="data:image/jpeg;base64," alt="CODESYS ladder logic and Factory I/O sorting line with Ignition HMI">
  <a href="https://docs.google.com/document/d/1mmCs3GJqyASBlRcrP2jAYdn6kR3t5fJLH9ij2ajK2vI/edit?usp=sharing" target="_blank" class="readmore">Read full write-up &rarr;</a>
</div>

<div class="project">
  <h3>Closed-Loop Liquid Level Control System</h3>
  <p class="meta">CODESYS Structured Text &middot; Factory I/O &middot; OPC UA &middot; PI Controller &middot; 2025</p>
  <p>
    Automatic fluid level control using CODESYS as the PLC and Factory I/O for the 3D
    simulation. OPC UA connects the two so code can read the level sensor and drive the
    valves in real time, the same way a real plant would be wired up.
  </p>
  <p>
    The logic is a state machine &mdash; Idle, Active, Paused. If you hit Stop, it
    closes the valves and freezes right where it is. Hit Start again and it picks up
    from the same spot instead of starting over.
  </p>
  <p>
    PI controller tuned to K<sub>p</sub>&nbsp;=&nbsp;2.2, T<sub>i</sub>&nbsp;=&nbsp;12s,
    targeting 50% fill. Skipped the derivative term because water ripples made it twitch
    the valve constantly. Output is capped at 10V. Tested with a 50% leak (held level)
    and a 100% leak (valve maxed out safely, nothing broke).
  </p>
  <img src="data:image/jpeg;base64," class="project-img" alt="CODESYS variable watch and Factory I/O 3D tank simulation">
  <a href="https://docs.google.com/document/d/16TDgdC-UNPQ4IIMzkM6556fIgYfGT61uA1mG8u1-dqA/edit?usp=sharing" target="_blank" class="readmore">Read full write-up &rarr;</a>
</div>

<div class="project">
  <h3>Automated Quadcopter</h3>
  <p class="meta">Python &middot; C++ &middot; OpenCV &middot; Arduino &middot; Raspberry Pi &middot; CNC / 3D Printing &middot; 2024&ndash;2025</p>
  <p>
    Built a quadcopter from scratch — frame is CNC-milled and 3D-printed, Arduino
    handles the flight loop, and a Raspberry Pi runs OpenCV for object and obstacle
    detection. The two boards talk over serial: the Pi figures out what it sees and
    passes suggested moves to the Arduino, which decides what to do based on the IMU.
  </p>
</div>

<div class="project">
  <h3>Submersible Trash-Collection System</h3>
  <p class="meta">Arduino &middot; Raspberry Pi &middot; Python &middot; Motor Control &middot; SkillsUSA WA State &middot; 2022&ndash;2023</p>
  <p>
    Led a four-person high school team on a submersible robot designed to pick up waste
    from the bottom of bodies of water. I owned the controls and navigation side of things
    and put together the technical presentation we took to the SkillsUSA Washington State
    competition.
  </p>
</div>

<!-- Skills -->
<h2>Skills</h2>
<table class="skills-table">
  <tr>
    <td>Programming</td>
    <td>Python, Java, JavaScript (intermediate) &middot; C/C++ on embedded hardware</td>
  </tr>
  <tr>
    <td>Hardware</td>
    <td>Arduino, Raspberry Pi &middot; Manual mill, CNC, 3D printing</td>
  </tr>
  <tr>
    <td>Controls &amp; PLC</td>
    <td>CODESYS &middot; IEC 61131-3 ladder logic &middot; Factory I/O &middot; OPC UA &middot; Ignition Perspective SCADA &middot; MATLAB &amp; Simulink</td>
  </tr>
  <tr>
    <td>Analysis</td>
    <td>Statistical inference, hypothesis testing, probability, data analysis</td>
  </tr>
</table>

<!-- Education -->
<h2>Education</h2>

<div class="edu-item">
  <div class="years">2024 &ndash; 2028</div>
  <div class="details">
    <h3>B.S. Electrical &amp; Computer Engineering</h3>
    <p>University of Washington &mdash; Seattle, WA &nbsp; <span class="gpa">GPA: 3.24</span></p>
    <p>Expected June 2028</p>
  </div>
</div>

<!-- Contact -->
<div class="contact-footer">
  <p><strong>Get in touch</strong></p>
  <p>
    <a href="mailto:lwah@uw.edu">lwah@uw.edu</a> &nbsp;&middot;&nbsp;
    <a href="tel:+15095086838">+1 509-508-6838</a> &nbsp;&middot;&nbsp;
  </p>
</div>

</body>
</html>
